wild for exactly this reason, and the whole CM6 community converged on CSS `Decoration.line`
instead. Only worth spending time on if Experiment 1's `padding-left`/`margin-left`
approach keeps showing cascade fragility against real themes despite the additive-only
discipline.

**Outcome.** Not triggered. Experiment 1 showed no cascade fragility against the synthetic corpus or
real vault notes once its three bugs were fixed
(see [08-experiment-1-additive-indentation.md](08-experiment-1-additive-indentation.md)) — nothing suggests the
`padding-left`/`margin-left` approach is running out of road.

## Verification infrastructure (Track 5 — applied across all experiments)

- Commit the fixture corpus once; every experiment's verification re-screenshots all of it,
  not just its own new fixture.
- One automated check: render two elements of deliberately different font-size (a heading
  line and a paragraph line) at the same computed tree depth, diff *every*
  decoration-related computed style at once (`padding-left`, `margin-left`, any marker
  `font-size`/`width`/`height`). This single mechanism would have caught both the
  indentation em/rem bug and the marker-size em/rem bug last time, instead of two separate
  manual catches.
- Fix `npm run build` to actually invoke esbuild, or add an explicit pre-verification step
  (`npm run build:plugin && npm run vault:install`) to the checklist so a stale bundle
  can't produce a false "verified" result again.
- A real dev-vault pass is part of *every* experiment's exit criteria, not a final step
  after all experiments conclude.

## Architecture question: per-block editor tree — resolved, not prototyped

Researched via the author's `continuous-journal` plugin as the concrete test case.
Verdict: not a lighter alternative to the already-rejected custom-view path — it requires a
comparable-or-larger private-API footprint (leaf-grafting, `setActiveLeaf` monkey-patching)
plus a genuinely unsolved new problem (N editors bound to overlapping ranges of one shared
file, kept consistent as any one of them edits). Shelved against this project's decided
100%-public-API bar (Q1); revisit only if that bar itself is ever renegotiated.

content.content:
1	# Experiment 5: per-kind block markers — 5a vs. 5b head to head (2026-07-16 → 2026-07-19)
2	
3	Part of the decoration-experiments series — see the
4	[hub](07-decoration-experiments-plan.md) for ground rules, fixture corpus, and the overall
5	results table. Proposed after [Experiment 2b](09-experiment-2-guide-lines.md) was kept:
6	not a revival of the deprioritized Experiment 3 (a legibility fallback), but a richer
7	visual system on top of 2b's guides. Both variants were built in parallel, in separate
8	worktrees/sessions, against the same corpus. **5a won the head-to-head** — see the
9	comparison and verdict section below, which also carries the hardening checklist for
10	taking 5a to production. Cross-experiment lessons:
11	[11-decoration-lessons.md](11-decoration-lessons.md).
12	
13	## Design (from the plan)
14	
15	Proposed after 2b was confirmed kept: not a revival of Experiment 3 (which was a
16	fallback for a legibility gap that turned out not to exist), but a richer visual
17	system — a distinct marker symbol per kind (heading, paragraph, code, table, callout,
18	quote, html, hr), now that 2b's guides give a stable, already-debugged foundation to
19	build markers on top of. Gets a fresh number rather than reusing 3, since 3's own
20	verdict is already recorded and settled. Build **both** below against the same corpus
21	and compare, same head-to-head discipline as Experiment 2 — don't pick a favorite in
22	advance.
23	
24	List items are excluded from markers entirely on both variants, for the same reason
25	guides already exclude list-item ancestors (see Experiment 2a's non-obvious findings
26	below): the native bullet/number already does this job.
27	
28	**Shared prerequisite, added independently on each branch** (trivial, and NOT itself
29	part of either technique being compared — analogous to the shared fixture corpus, not
30	to the guide-computation code 2a/2b each built separately): `LineDecorationFact`
31	(`decorate.ts`) doesn't carry node kind today, only structural buckets
32	(`isAtom`/`isListItem`/else) — add a `kind: NodeKind` field, populated straight from
33	`node.kind` in the existing walk. No new tree walk or pure function needed at all:
34	`isFirstLine` (already present) is exactly the right gate for "does this line get a
35	marker" — simpler than either 2a's or 2b's own guide computation, both of which needed
36	a second pass. Also: promote the one-off plain-blockquote fixture (`quote` kind,
37	currently only inline in `51-guides-gradient.e2e.ts` as `decorations-guide-
38	blockquote.md`, never in the shared `ALL_DECORATION_FIXTURES` corpus) into
39	`e2e/fixtures/decorations.ts` proper — it's the one kind of the eight that ground rule
40	#2's "screenshot everything, every time" has never actually covered.
41	
42	**A real constraint, not a preference, is why the two variants use genuinely different
43	mechanisms rather than two flavors of the same trick**: CSS gives each line exactly two
44	pseudo-elements, and both are already spoken for. Native blockquote uses `::before` for
45	its colored bar (confirmed in Experiment 2b; callout likely too, given how visually
46	similar its own bar is, but not yet confirmed live — don't assume). 2b's guides already
47	use `::after`. Neither variant can claim a third pseudo-element that doesn't exist.
48	
49	### 5a — icon markers, via a new DOM-element mechanism
50	
51	Small, fixed-size (rem/px, **never `em`** — the exact historical marker-size bug class
52	from the postmortem: `font-size` resolving against the wrong line's context) SVG icon
53	per kind, built via DOM APIs directly (no data-URI encoding needed) — one distinct,
54	self-drawn mark per eligible kind. Not a final design; exact icon shapes get tuned by
55	eye during real-vault review, like every other visual call in this project.
56	
57	**Mechanism**: plain `.cm-line`s (heading/paragraph/code/quote) get a CM6
58	`Decoration.widget` (`side: -1`) whose `toDOM()` returns a `position: absolute` element
59	— deliberately out of flow, so it can't push text or trigger the `.cm-widgetBuffer`
60	line-wrap risk Experiment 4 flagged (that risk was specifically about *in-flow* spacer
61	widgets). Widget-replaced atoms (table/callout/html/hr) extend the existing
62	`MarginCompensation` `ViewPlugin`, which already walks these elements post-render via
63	`docViewUpdate` and patches them directly, to also inject/update a small marker child
64	element — the same proven escape hatch already used for `margin-left`, now attaching a
65	real node instead of just an inline style. `pointer-events: none` throughout, matching
66	the guide's own `::after`.
67	
68	Positioning needs to be *verified live*, not reasoned from the box model:
69	`padding-left` lines (heading/paragraph) need the marker inside the reserved padding
70	gutter (a negative offset back toward the box's own left edge); `margin-left` lines
71	(atoms) already have their box shifted, so the marker sits near the box's own edge
72	instead. Confirm via `getBoundingClientRect()` against a reference point (e.g. a
73	same-depth list item's own native bullet) — this project has been burned before by
74	trusting box-model reasoning over live measurement (2b's own `::after`-widening
75	finding).
76	
77	**What would kill it**: if an out-of-flow `Decoration.widget` still trips the
78	`.cm-widgetBuffer` gotcha in some way that matters even without pushing text: go in
79	expecting this might need real investigation, same posture Experiment 4 took toward the
80	identical CM6 mechanism.
81	
82	**Success criteria**: every eligible kind's marker renders on the node's true first
83	line only (via `isFirstLine`) — never repeated on continuation lines, never on
84	trailing-gap lines. No stacking/paint-order conflict with the guide's `::after` or
85	native `::before`. Unaffected by `contain: paint` on widget atoms (a prepended child
86	sits inside the box, unlike the guide's deliberately-widened pseudo).
87	
88	### 5b — CSS-shape markers, reusing 2b's existing background-layer mechanism
89	
90	Small fixed-px CSS-gradient-drawn geometric marks — filled dot, hollow ring, filled
91	square, diamond (`conic-gradient`), plus-sign (layered `linear-gradient`s), short
92	bar/tick, wedge, cross — one shape+color combination per kind. Zero new
93	pseudo-element, zero new DOM, zero new `ViewPlugin`.
94	
95	**Mechanism**: one more compound background-layer string per kind (self-contained
96	`background-position`/`background-size`/`no-repeat`, same pattern `guideLayer()`
97	already uses), exposed as a new `--to-marker` custom property — kept as an independent
98	function from the guide layers in the TS code, but painted via the *same* `::after` by
99	changing the CSS rule's `background` shorthand to `background: var(--to-marker,
100	none), var(--to-guides, none);`. Positioned via the exact same `--to-own-shift`
101	compensation already proven for guides — no new coordinate math.
102	
103	**Real integration wrinkle, not just an additive change**: today the `to-decor-guides`
104	class — which gates `position: relative` plus the whole `::after` rule — is applied
105	only when `guide.guideDepths.length > 0`. Markers need to appear even on a depth-0
106	heading with zero ancestors (no guide at all today), so the gate becomes
107	`guideDepths.length > 0 || hasMarker`. This touches shared, working, already-well-
108	tested code (`lineDecoration()`, used by both the plain-line `StateField` and the
109	widget-atom `MarginCompensation` loop) — change it carefully. `gapLineDecoration`
110	(blank trailing-gap lines) must stay explicitly marker-free, since gap lines carry no
111	`decorate()` fact or node kind at all.
112	
113	**Success criteria**: same as 5a's, achieved with zero new rendering mechanism.
114	
115	### Compare on
116	
117	Visual legibility/distinctiveness of the 8 marks at a glance — the actual question
118	this head-to-head exists to answer, not a secondary concern. Code size/complexity/new-
119	mechanism risk (5b reuses proven plumbing entirely; 5a introduces a new DOM/widget
120	mechanism with its own unverified risk, similar in kind to Experiment 4's).
121	Robustness across the full corpus, multi-line continuation, and widget atoms.
122	Cascade/paint safety. Record the result in the comparison table below before picking
123	one (or keeping both, if the answer turns out to be "different marks for different
124	kinds").
125	
126	## Results — Experiment 5a: icon markers (DOM-element mechanism)
127	
128	**Status: done, corpus + real-vault coverage confirmed.** Shared prerequisite added
129	first: `kind: NodeKind` on `LineDecorationFact` (`decorate.ts`), plus a unit test
130	confirming it at every line including list-item continuations
131	([tests/decorate.test.ts](../../tests/decorate.test.ts)); the plain-blockquote
132	fixture promoted into `ALL_DECORATION_FIXTURES` as `quote`
133	([e2e/fixtures/decorations.ts](../../e2e/fixtures/decorations.ts)), so the full
134	8-kind set is now covered by the shared "screenshot everything" loop.
135	
136	**Mechanism, as planned**: a small, distinct, self-drawn SVG icon per kind (heading
137	"H", paragraph text-lines, code `</>`, table grid, callout alert-circle, quote
138	opening-marks, html tag-with-fold, hr bar), built via DOM APIs (`createElementNS` +
139	attribute setting), never a data-URI. Two delivery mechanisms, split the same way
140	indentation/guides already are: a CM6 `Decoration.widget` (`side: -1`,
141	`position: absolute`, out of flow) in a **separate StateField** from the existing
142	line-decoration one (sidesteps any need to reason about `Decoration.line`/
143	`Decoration.widget` ordering at equal positions — CM6 merges multiple StateFields'
144	decorations correctly on its own) for plain lines (heading/paragraph/code/quote);
145	a direct DOM child injected by the existing `MarginCompensation` `ViewPlugin` for
146	widget-replaced atoms (table/callout/html/hr), the same proven escape hatch already
147	used for their `margin-left` (a CM6 decoration has zero effect on these, confirmed
148	by Experiment 1/2b).
149	
150	**A real design decision beyond the handoff prompt's literal wording**: the prompt
151	didn't specify how much extra space a marker needs vs. the *existing* depth-based
152	indentation. Reusing the existing gutter (marker inside already-reserved padding)
153	doesn't work at depth 0 — flat/top-level nodes have zero padding, so there's no
154	gutter to draw into, yet the whole point of Experiment 5 is a marker on every
155	eligible kind at every depth, depth 0 included. Resolved by reserving a **new,
156	small, fixed (`rem`, never `em`) `--to-marker-gutter` additively on top of the
157	existing depth formula**, for every non-list-item line (continuation lines
158	included, so text stays aligned across a whole multi-line node, not just its own
159	first line) — list items reserve nothing (no marker, native bullet/number
160	untouched). This makes the marker's absolute column agree exactly between the two
161	mechanisms: block lines (padding-left, box not shifted) place the icon at
162	`left: depth * unit` (the start of the new gutter); atom lines — both plain
163	(margin-left) and widget-replaced — place it at `left: -gutter` relative to their
164	own (already gutter-shifted) box, landing at the identical global column. Verified
165	live via rect/computed-style assertions, not reasoned from the box model alone (see
166	`e2e/specs/52-block-markers-icons.e2e.ts`).
167	
168	**Two real integration risks found and fixed, both by extending existing,
169	already-hardened machinery rather than reasoning from scratch**:
170	
171	1. `contain: paint` (Obsidian's own containment hint on widget-replaced atoms,
172	   already fought once for guides) also clips a real DOM child positioned outside
173	   its own box — not just a pseudo-element background. The existing override was
174	   gated on `.to-decor-guides` (only present when an ancestor owns a guide), but a
175	   marker is present on **every** widget atom, guide or not — a depth-0 table with
176	   no ancestor would have had its marker silently clipped. Fixed by widening the
177	   gate to `.to-decor-guides, .to-decor-marker`, and adding a regression test
178	   specifically for the no-ancestor case
179	   (`no !important/specificity or contain:paint regression: a depth-0 table...`).
180	2. The guide's own `--to-own-shift` compensation (2b) had to grow to include the
181	   new marker gutter for atom lines (both plain and widget-replaced), since their
182	   own box is now shifted right by `depth * unit + gutter`, not just `depth * unit`
183	   — otherwise a guide on a marker-bearing atom line would land one gutter-width
184	   short of the correct ancestor column. Block lines needed no change (padding
185	   never shifts the box, so their own-shift stays 0 regardless of the gutter).
186	
187	**Full test suite** (`npm test` + `npm run test:e2e`, 8 e2e spec files, 44 tests)
188	green after the change, including two **pre-existing** Experiment 1/2b tests that
189	needed deliberate updates (not silently patched to match, per the postmortem's own
190	warning about that failure mode) because the underlying behavior legitimately
191	changed: a depth-0 heading's `padding-left` is no longer `0px` (now the marker
192	gutter, since every non-list block reserves one even at depth 0), and a same-depth
193	list item vs. code fence no longer land at the same column (the code fence, an
194	atom, reserves a marker gutter; the list item, excluded from markers, doesn't) —
195	both changes are documented inline at the updated assertions with the reasoning
196	above.
197	
198	**Real-vault-equivalent pass**: `npm run vault:install` succeeded (symlinks in
199	place, plugin bundle built via `build:plugin`, not just type-checked). This
200	environment has no interactive access to the author's own Obsidian instance, so the
201	closest available check — the same one prior experiments on this project used as
202	their "real (non-synthetic) vault notes" proxy — is screenshotting the bundled
203	`test-vault`'s own real journal/notes/README content (not the synthetic fixture
204	corpus) through the e2e harness, both themes. Reviewed by eye:
205	multi-line-wrapped paragraphs get exactly one marker on their true first line
206	(never repeated on wrapped continuation rows); a callout with mixed checkbox
207	siblings, wikilinks, and a table all render correctly together with no clipping,
208	overlap, or stacking conflict; dark theme unaffected. A genuine pass by the
209	author's own hand against their personal vault is still the stronger bar the
210	postmortem asks for and hasn't happened yet for this specific branch.
211	
212	**Code cost**: ~180 lines added to `decorations.ts` (icon builder switch + 8 SVG
213	shapes, `MarkerWidget`, `computeMarkers`, widget-atom marker injection/cleanup
214	helpers), ~35 lines in `styles.css` (gutter reservation + `position: relative` +
215	extended `contain`/`overflow` override gate), ~10 lines in `decorate.ts` (the
216	shared `kind` field prerequisite).
217	
218	**Follow-up round 1: fold-indicator/marker crowding, found in review.** A
219	heading's native collapse chevron (`.cm-fold-indicator .collapse-indicator`) is
220	inserted at essentially the same "line start" anchor the marker's own target
221	column is defined relative to — its default position overlapped the marker at
222	every heading level, and (unlike a list item, where the native chevron already
223	sits well left of the bullet) a heading's chevron rendered to the marker's
224	*right* instead, an inconsistent layout. Fixed by repositioning the chevron via
225	`transform: translateX(...)` to sit left of both the marker and (when present) a
226	shallower ancestor's own guide column. Two rounds of live correction went into
227	the fix: measuring against the WRAPPER (`.collapse-indicator`, 22px wide)
228	instead of the actual painted `<svg>` glyph (~10px, centered inside with ~6px of
229	invisible hit-area padding per side) made a fit look impossible at deeper
230	nesting, where an ancestor's own guide is also active on the same row — it
231	wasn't; the wrapper can't avoid both neighbors in the available space, but the
232	glyph comfortably can. Also needed testing against a genuinely nested fixture
233	(3 heading levels deep), not a flat 2-level one — the collision this guards
234	against only appears when a shallower ancestor's guide line is also active on
235	the same row. See `e2e/specs/52-block-markers-icons.e2e.ts`'s "native fold
236	chevron glyph sits between the marker and an ancestor's guide line, clear of
237	both."
238	
239	**Follow-up round 2: marker placement — three variants explored, settled on
240	centered.** Post-review, tried three candidates for WHERE the icon sits
241	relative to the shared guide-line column, each wired as a real (temporary,
242	debug-only) plugin setting so they could be compared live against a real vault
243	without a rebuild per attempt: (A) icon's own left edge at the column; (B) icon
244	horizontally centered on the column; (C) no marker gutter reserved at all —
245	text stays at the exact no-marker column, and the icon's own right edge sits in
246	whatever whitespace already exists to the left (none at depth 0, by
247	construction). B read best in real-vault comparison and was kept permanently;
248	the setting itself (and A/C) were removed once the choice was made — unlike the
249	visibility setting below, placement was never meant to be a permanent, shipped
250	axis of configurability, just a way to compare candidates without a rebuild per
251	attempt.
252	
253	**Follow-up round 3: leaf-node marker visibility — kept as a real, permanent
254	setting.** A review observation after living with 5a for a while: a marker reads
255	well as a crown on top of the guide line, indicating a BRANCH node's kind, but
256	adds comparatively little for a LEAF — most leaf atom kinds (code, table,
257	callout, quote, html, hr) already carry their own native visual style (a code
258	fence's background, a callout's colored bar, etc.), so a marker there can read
259	as pure distraction. Added `markerVisibility` (`'all'` / `'with-children'` /
260	`'headings-and-paragraphs'`) as a genuine, persisted, user-facing setting
261	(`mode-registry.ts`) — not a temporary debug toggle like the placement
262	exploration above, since which nodes get a marker at all turned out to be a
263	legitimate, ongoing matter of taste the original plan didn't anticipate needing
264	to stay configurable. `'with-children'` hides markers on any leaf, atoms
265	included (`hasChildren` is a new, pure `LineDecorationFact` field:
266	`node.children.length > 0`). `'headings-and-paragraphs'` instead keys off KIND,
267	not per-instance state: the only two kinds that can *ever* have children in
268	this tree model — atoms are leaves by construction, and list items are already
269	excluded from markers unconditionally — so `!fact.isAtom` exactly captures it.
270	
271	A real, separate bug was found and fixed while wiring this setting's live-
272	refresh path: `computeMarkers`/`computeDecorations` both skip widget-replaced
273	atoms entirely (`MarginCompensation` owns their marker instead), so for a note
274	containing *only* widget atoms (e.g. a lone table), changing `markerVisibility`
275	produced byte-identical decoration output before and after — CM6 correctly saw
276	no diff and never re-fired `MarginCompensation`'s `docViewUpdate` hook, silently
277	failing to update the table's marker until the next full mode toggle. The
278	existing mode-toggle refresh (a plain cursor nudge) doesn't reliably reach a
279	ViewPlugin with no decorations of its own, for exactly this reason. Fixed by
280	having the setting's own setter toggle outline mode off then immediately back
281	on (via the registry directly, not the user-facing command) — guaranteeing two
282	genuinely different decoration outputs (none vs. the real thing) that CM6
283	always detects, reliably re-triggering `docViewUpdate` twice. This also
284	retroactively fixed the identical latent gap in the earlier placement setting
285	(round 2), which no test happened to exercise against a table-only note.
286	
287	**Follow-up round 4: a genuine architectural bug, found via a flaky test —
288	decorations leaking into Obsidian's own nested per-cell editors.** While
289	verifying the visibility setting's e2e coverage, one specific test (visibility
290	`'headings-and-paragraphs'` against a table) failed intermittently — first
291	misdiagnosed as an async timing race in the table widget's own DOM settling (a
292	`browser.pause()` was gambling against however long that took) and "fixed"
293	with a poll-based wait instead of a fixed sleep, which is a legitimate,
294	permanent improvement in its own right (`waitForContentChildCount` in
295	`e2e/helpers.ts`, mirroring the existing `waitForNotice` pattern) but did *not*
296	fix the underlying test — it kept failing deterministically once actual
297	machine load (which had been intermittently masking/unmasking it across runs)
298	settled down enough to reproduce reliably.
299	
300	Root-caused by tracing the stray marker element's full DOM ancestry: Obsidian
301	renders an actively-edited table cell (cursor inside it) as its own separate,
302	independent CM6 `EditorView`, mounted inside the outer table widget's own DOM
303	(`.cm-embed-block.cm-table-widget` → `.table-wrapper` → `<table>` → `<tr>` →
304	`<th>`/`<td>` → `.table-cell-wrapper` → a whole nested `.cm-editor`). This
305	plugin's `registerEditorExtension` (`main.ts`) applies its decorations to
306	*every* CM6 instance Obsidian creates app-wide — this nested one included — and
307	that nested editor's own "document" is just the cell's raw text (e.g. a single
308	word), which `decorate()`/`parse()` classifies as a plain paragraph (the
309	default block kind for a bare line with no special syntax) — not an atom, so
310	under `'all'`/`'headings-and-paragraphs'` visibility it becomes marker-eligible
311	and picks up both a stray marker icon *and* depth-based padding/margin exactly
312	like a real top-level line, visibly corrupting the cell being edited. This is a
313	real, live, user-facing bug, not just a test artifact — confirmed independently
314	by the author noticing it in their own use before this was reported back.
315	
316	The state-only "is this note in outline mode" gate (`editorInfoField`, already
317	used throughout `decorations.ts` as a reliable gate everywhere else) cannot
318	distinguish the nested editor from the real top-level one — confirmed live via
319	`EditorView.findFromDOM()` on the nested instance that its own `editorInfoField`
320	resolves to the exact *same* outer `MarkdownView` object as the real note. Only
321	DOM ancestry can tell them apart: a real top-level note's own `.cm-editor` is
322	never itself nested inside a `.cm-embed-block` (those are its own descendants,
323	never its ancestors). Since `StateField.create`/`update` have no view/DOM
324	access at all, fixing this required moving both decoration `StateField`s
325	(`computeDecorations`/`computeMarkers`) to `ViewPlugin`s (which do have
326	`view.dom`), gating each — and `MarginCompensation`, already a ViewPlugin — on
327	a shared `isNestedEditor(view)` check (`view.dom.closest('.cm-embed-block') !==
328	null`). Confirmed fixed via the same DOM-ancestry trace, and via two
329	consecutive clean e2e runs of the affected test (previously flaky, now
330	deterministic) plus the full 8-spec-file suite (58 tests) twice with no
331	regressions.
332	
333	**Code cost of rounds 2-4 combined**: ~20 lines in `decorate.ts`
334	(`hasChildren`), ~15 lines in `mode-registry.ts` (`MarkerVisibility` type +
335	setting), ~15 lines in `main.ts` (setter + the off/on `forceRedraw` toggle +
336	settings dropdown), net ~+85 lines in `decorations.ts` after removing the
337	placement-variant machinery (round 2) and adding the nested-editor guard (round
338	4: two new small `ViewPlugin` wrapper classes replacing two `StateField`s, plus
339	`isNestedEditor`/`shouldShowMarker`).
340	
341	## Results — Experiment 5b: CSS-shape markers (background-layer mechanism)
342	
343	**Verdict: keep**, pending the head-to-head against 5a. Branched off
344	`experiment/decorations-2b-guides-css-gradient`. All 9 corpus fixtures (the shared 8, plus
345	a `quote`-kind fixture promoted into `ALL_DECORATION_FIXTURES` per this experiment's own
346	shared prerequisite) screenshotted, both bundled themes, plus targeted computed-style
347	assertions on the resolved `::after` background-image — see
348	`e2e/specs/52-block-markers-shapes.e2e.ts` (on the closed 5b branch
349	`claude/decorations-block-markers-595e13` — 5b's code never merged),
350	71/71 e2e tests green across the full suite (all 8 spec files, including a dedicated
351	"marker vertical position" sub-suite added after real-vault review — see bug 5 below,
352	two pixel-exact guide/marker-alignment tests, and a fold-chevron-clearance sub-suite —
353	see the follow-ups below), plus 127/127 unit tests (`npm test`), including new
354	`decorate: kind`/`headingGuideDepths` tests. Confirmed live
355	on 4 real vault notes (headings, paragraphs, lists, checkboxes, wikilinks, a code block, a
356	callout, a table) — no defects found; markers coexist cleanly with real content, including
357	multi-line paragraphs and wikilinks immediately after a marked line.
358	
359	**Design pivoted once, after a live look at the first version.** The plan's own design
360	called for a distinct shape+color per eligible kind (8 marks: dot/ring/square/diamond/
361	plus/tick/wedge/cross across Obsidian's 8 accent colors) — built, screenshotted, and
362	initially judged reasonable from a distance. On closer real-content review our own
363	verdict was that the variety read as **cryptic, not helpful**: eight different shapes and
364	colors ask a reader to memorize a legend before the marks mean anything, which is the
365	opposite of legible. Replaced with a single, uniform, solid dot — same color as the guide
366	lines themselves (`var(--text-faint)`) — for every eligible kind. This is a real design
367	lesson worth carrying forward past this one experiment: for a piece of chrome whose whole
368	job is "signal that a node starts here," uniformity communicates that job better than
369	variety that has no established meaning yet. The per-kind shape system is described below
370	only for the record (what was tried and why it was reverted), not as the current design —
371	current code has exactly one shape.
372	
373	**Mechanism (current)**: a single `radial-gradient` dot (`markerBackground()` in
374	`decorations.ts`), same color as guides, one call site for every eligible kind (heading/
375	paragraph/code/table/callout/quote/html/hr — everything except list-item, which needs no
376	marker check at all now: eligibility collapsed to "not a list item," full stop, since
377	every other `NodeKind` gets the identical mark). Exposed as a new `--to-marker` custom
378	property, painted through the *exact* same `::after` 2b's guides already use
379	(`background: var(--to-marker, none), var(--to-guides, none);`) — zero new
380	pseudo-element, zero new DOM, zero new `ViewPlugin`. Gap lines stay explicitly
381	marker-free (they carry no `decorate()` fact/kind at all — verified by a dedicated e2e
382	test).
383	
384	**The real integration wrinkle, exactly as flagged going in**: `lineDecoration()`'s
385	`to-decor-guides` gate (driving `position: relative` and the whole `::after` rule) was
386	guide-only (`guideDepths.length > 0`); changed to `guideDepths.length > 0 || hasMarker` so
387	a depth-0 node with zero ancestors (e.g. a bare top-level heading, which has no guide of
388	its own today) still shows a marker — confirmed live and by a dedicated e2e test
389	(`getLinePseudoComputedStyle` reads a non-empty resolved background at depth 0). This
390	touches the shared `MarginCompensation` `ViewPlugin` too (for widget-rendered atoms:
391	table/callout/html/hr), which needed the identical gate change plus setting `--to-marker`
392	alongside its existing `--to-guides` handling.
393	
394	**Bugs found and fixed** (all caught only by actually looking at a rendered screenshot,
395	not by any DOM/computed-style assertion — directly reinforcing this project's recurring
396	lesson that visual claims need an actual look, not box-model reasoning alone):
397	
398	1. **Depth-0 markers were completely invisible, caught by zooming into a screenshot of
399	   the `mixed` fixture's top-level `"# Top"` heading.** The marker's `background-position`
400	   is deliberately negative at shallow depths (it sits `MARKER_GAP` left of the node's own
401	   indent column, which is column 0 at depth 0) — but a background can *never* paint
402	   outside its own element's box, regardless of what any ancestor's `overflow`/`contain`
403	   allows. This is a **stricter** rule than the one guides rely on: guides' own doc comment
404	   established that a pseudo's box can be *widened* via `left`/`right` to reach a shallower
405	   column, but a first pass here wrongly assumed the same finding covered a marker's own
406	   small negative position too, without the box actually being widened for it. Fixed by
407	   computing `markerShortfall(depth)` — `max(0px, MARKER_RESERVE − depth × unit)`, the same
408	   `max(0px, ...)` clamp idiom Experiment 1's own table-padding fix established — and
409	   folding it into `--to-own-shift` (which now activates whenever `ownShiftUnits > 0 ||
410	   marker`, not just for margin-shifted kinds) *and* into every guide layer's own position
411	   formula active on the same line (`guideLayer` gained an `extra` parameter), since the
412	   pseudo's local coordinate origin is one shared thing for the whole box — widening it for
413	   the marker's sake shifts everything else painted in that same box too. At this module's
414	   default sizes, only depth 0 actually needs the extra reach — confirmed live that every
415	   depth ≥ 1 marker rendered correctly even before this fix, which is exactly why the bug
416	   surfaced only on the flat/mixed fixtures' top-level nodes and not on any nested one.
417	2. **(Per-kind design, since reverted) The table's diamond mark rendered as a lopsided
418	   pinwheel**, caught by zooming into a screenshot of the `widget-atoms` fixture — the
419	   standard 4-gradient diamond recipe requires every quadrant's gradient to list
420	   `transparent 50%` before the color, consistently across all four; an earlier version
421	   flipped that order on two of the four quadrants, producing a shape with the wrong
422	   halves filled.
423	3. **(Per-kind design, since reverted) The callout's plus-sign rendered as a lopsided "⊥"**
424	   (vertical bar with the crossbar near its bottom, not its middle), caught by zooming into
425	   the same `widget-atoms` screenshot. Root cause: the vertical-centering formula used `%`,
426	   and `background-position`'s `%` component resolves relative to *that specific layer's
427	   own image size* subtracted from the box — which differed between the vertical bar (a
428	   full-height image) and the horizontal bar (a much thinner one), so the "same" `50%`
429	   position landed at two different absolute heights for the two layers making up one
430	   shape. Invisible on single-line plain `.cm-line`s (box height ≈ marker size, so the
431	   discrepancy was too small to notice) but glaring on a widget atom whose box spans its
432	   *entire* rendered height (a multi-line callout, not just its title line).
433	4. **Heading markers sat noticeably above the native fold chevron on H1–H5, and merely
434	   collided with it on H6 — reported directly from a real-vault look, not
435	   caught by any fixture or assertion.** The fix for bug 3 above replaced `%`-based
436	   vertical centering with a FIXED length from the box's own top, chosen to solve the
437	   widget-atom case — but a fixed length doesn't scale with a *heading's own* line-height,
438	   which varies by level (H1's line box is taller than H6's). Measured live, not guessed:
439	   `.cm-fold-indicator`'s own `getBoundingClientRect()` against its line's, for H1 through
440	   H6 in a real running Obsidian instance. Two findings: the chevron's own vertical
441	   *center* sits at a level-dependent ~67–70% down the line box (not a clean, reusable
442	   constant), but its **top** is a robust, level-independent constant — exactly 1rem below
443	   the line's own top at every level measured — because the chevron's position is fixed
444	   while only *its own icon size* (not its position) scales with the heading's font-size.
445	   First fix: gave heading lines specifically a measured fixed-top anchor (`1rem`, matching
446	   the chevron's own measured top). Confirmed live via screenshot — no longer "above" on
447	   any level — but this fix didn't survive the NEXT round of real-vault review (bug 5).
448	5. **The whole "one CSS formula per box-type" approach was still wrong — a full-suite
449	   real-vault review found FIVE distinct placement failures at once, all reported directly
450	   by manual review, none caught by any fixture/assertion.** Headings still sat above their own
451	   text (the bug-4 fixed-top anchor didn't move with a font-size-driven line-height
452	   *increase* the same way the chevron's OWN top didn't, but our own read of "middle
453	   of the heading text" wanted the TEXT's center, and a bare fixed length can't track a
454	   font-size it has no knowledge of at layout time); single-line paragraphs sat at their own
455	   top edge (the still-present double-`HALF`-subtraction bug from bug 3, never actually
456	   fixed for the plain-line case, only worked around for widget atoms); wrapped multi-row
457	   paragraphs/blockquotes centered on their ENTIRE wrapped height instead of just the first
458	   visual row; callouts and code blocks sat at the literal top of the whole block instead of
459	   near their own first content row. Root cause, named directly in review: **no CSS-only
460	   formula (percentage, fixed length, or any mix) can know where "the first rendered visual
461	   row of this specific box's content" actually is** — that's fundamentally a *rendering*
462	   fact, not a static layout constant, and every earlier fix in this area was really just a
463	   differently-shaped guess at it. Fixed by abandoning CSS-only positioning for `Y` entirely
464	   and measuring it live: `MarginCompensation` (already the established "StateField draws a
465	   default, ViewPlugin patches in the true live-read value" mechanism for `nativeMarginBasePx`
466	   and table-padding compensation) now computes a real per-line pixel offset via
467	   `document.createRange().selectNodeContents(lineEl); range.getClientRects()[0]` — the DOM's
468	   own notion of an element's first wrapped visual row, which handles word-wrap for free and
469	   scales with whatever font-size is actually in effect, no guessing required. Written to a
470	   new `--to-decor-marker-y` custom property; `markerBackground()` itself only ever emits a
471	   `var(--to-decor-marker-y, 50%)` reference (the `50%` fallback is now CORRECTLY centering,
472	   not double-subtracted, for the brief window before the ViewPlugin's own pass overrides
473	   it). Widget atoms get the same technique aimed at a kind-appropriate reference element
474	   instead of the widget's own root (a callout's `.callout-title`, a table's first `<tr>`;
475	   `hr` has no text content at all, so its own half-height stands in; `html`, arbitrary raw
476	   markup with no fixed structure, falls back to the same first-row Range technique applied
477	   to its own root). One deliberate, documented exception: a code fence's structural first
478	   `.cm-line` is the OPENING marker row (confirmed live to be a REAL, normal-height row —
479	   not collapsed to zero — that Live Preview just renders with nothing but a language badge
480	   on it, with the actual code text starting on the NEXT line down) — reaching into that next
481	   line for a "true" measurement was rejected because this pseudo-element's own box is
482	   exactly ONE line tall and a background can never paint outside its own box (the same hard
483	   rule bug 1 already established), so doing this safely would need the same kind of
484	   vertical box-widening `--to-own-shift` already does horizontally, introducing an unverified
485	   NEW paint-order risk against the next (opaque-backgrounded) code line. Per an explicit review
486	   decision (accepting a slightly-lower offset over the harder exact calculation), code
487	   fences instead bias toward the bottom of their own (still fully in-bounds, zero clipping
488	   risk) opener row via a plain `95%` — confirmed live via computed style (`background-position:
489	   12px 95%`) to land within ~1px of the next line's own top, a pragmatic near-miss rather than
490	   an exact, mechanism-stretching alignment. All five cases now covered by dedicated e2e tests
491	   (see `52-block-markers-shapes.e2e.ts`'s "marker vertical position" suite) reading the
492	   resolved `background-position` directly, not just the raw custom property.
493	
494	**Code cost (current, single-dot design + live-measured Y)**: ~110 lines added to
495	`decorations.ts` (marker position/color constants, `markerBackground()`, `firstRowCenterPx`/
496	`widgetMarkerYPx`, and the `lineDecoration()`/`MarginCompensation` integration — still
497	meaningfully SMALLER than the original 8-shape version's ~150 lines despite the added
498	live-measurement code, since one shape needs no per-kind switch, no quadrant/tick/bar helper
499	functions, and no color table), ~1 line changed in `styles.css` (the `::after` rule's
500	`background` shorthand, plus an expanded doc comment), ~10 lines added to `decorate.ts`
501	(`kind` field on `LineDecorationFact` — still used by `MarginCompensation`'s widget-Y
502	dispatch and the code-fence Y exception, even though `markerBackground()` itself no longer
503	branches on kind). Reuses 100% of 2b's existing plumbing (custom property → single
504	`::after`, `--to-own-shift` compensation, `MarginCompensation`'s widget-DOM patching loop)
505	for X-axis positioning and shape/color — no new CM6 mechanism, no new pseudo-element, no
506	new `ViewPlugin`. Y-axis positioning is a GENUINELY new piece of live-measurement code
507	(not reused from guides, which never needed it), the first real net-new mechanism this
508	experiment added rather than reused. The one real *design* cost independent of Y (not
509	code-size cost) is that `--to-own-shift` and every guide layer's X position formula are no
510	longer independent of whether a marker is *also* active on the same line — a coupling 5a's
511	separate-DOM-element mechanism wouldn't have, since a real DOM child element doesn't need
512	to share a single pseudo-element's one coordinate space with the guide gradient.
513	
514	**Known limitation, not fixed on this branch**: markers and guides share one `::after`'s
515	single `opacity: 0.6`, inherited from the guide rule (subtle-on-purpose, for a background
516	grid). A marker dot at 60% opacity is legible but visibly softer than surrounding text —
517	CSS has no way to give two background layers on the *same* element independent opacity.
518	Splitting them (e.g. painting the marker at full opacity some other way) would cost exactly
519	the "claim a third pseudo-element" move this experiment's own design explicitly ruled out
520	as unavailable. Worth a deliberate follow-up decision (accept the shared opacity, or
521	promote markers off `::after` onto a real DOM node as 5a already does) once both variants
522	are compared side by side.
523	
524	**Follow-up refinement: guide columns now align with a marker's own CENTER, not the raw
525	depth boundary**, requested in review to read more like native nested lists (a connecting
526	line running straight through each bullet, not stopping short of it). Previously a guide at
527	ancestor depth `d` was drawn at column `d * unit` exactly — the same column the NEXT depth
528	level's indentation starts at, which is also where a marker's own reach calculation
529	happened to START (its left edge), not where it visually CENTERS. This left a real, visible
530	gap: the guide line sat to the right of the marker dot above/below it, a seam rather than a
531	continuous line. Fixed by shifting every guide's own column left by `MARKER_HALF +
532	MARKER_GAP` (`GUIDE_COLUMN_OFFSET`) — exactly the offset between a marker's own depth
533	column and its rendered center (confirmed live, pixel-exact: a dedicated e2e test reads
534	both a heading's own marker center and its descendant's guide column as independent
535	absolute screen positions — accounting for the pseudo-element's own `left` CSS offset
536	*and* `background-position`, a real bug in the test's first draft, not the implementation —
537	and asserts they match within 1px).
538	
539	This shift itself went negative for shallow (in practice, depth-0) ancestors, the same
540	"background can't paint outside its own box" constraint markers already ran into — fixed by
541	generalizing the existing `markerShortfall`/`--to-own-shift` widening mechanism
542	(`combineExtra`) to also cover the shallowest active guide's own reach, not just a line's
543	own marker. The two are independent reasons a line's pseudo box might need widening (its
544	OWN marker's left edge; a shallow ancestor's now marker-center-aligned guide column) that
545	happen to share one box, so `combineExtra` takes the `max()` of whichever apply. This also
546	meant a blank trailingGap line (which never carries a marker, only ever a guide) can now
547	independently need `--to-own-shift` too — previously true only for a small minority of
548	gap lines with genuinely shallow guides, but the mechanism generalizes without needing a
549	gap-specific carve-out.
550	
551	**Real bug shipped in the above fix, caught by manual review from a real-vault screenshot
552	(not by the dedicated alignment test) — mid-chain markers drifted visibly left of their
553	own guide column.** The screenshot showed a real note (`Wednesday — review day` → `Aurora
554	review` → paragraphs): the FIRST guide (from the top-level heading) correctly passed
555	through the second level's marker, but every level from there down drifted — the report correctly
556	diagnosed the guides themselves as evenly spaced and fine, and the markers (from the
557	second level on) as the ones shifted left. Root cause: the fix above computes ONE combined `extra` per
558	line — `max` of the line's own marker shortfall and its shallowest active guide's shortfall
559	— and that combined value is what the box is ACTUALLY widened by (`--to-own-shift`). The
560	marker's own X-position formula must use that SAME combined value for the cancellation
561	algebra to hold (`--to-own-shift` and the position formula need to widen/shift by identical
562	amounts, or the difference leaks into the rendered position) — but the code built the
563	marker's background string from its own (narrower) `markerShortfall` alone, computed
564	*before* combining with the guide's requirement, then combined separately only for
565	`--to-own-shift`. For a depth-0 node this is invisible (no guide of its own exists to
566	diverge from, so the two values coincide) — exactly the case the dedicated alignment test
567	used, which is why it passed despite the bug. For a MID-chain node like "Aurora review"
568	(depth 1, carries both its own marker AND an active depth-0 guide bridging through it),
569	its own shortfall alone is 0 (depth 1 doesn't need widening for its OWN marker), but the
570	combined value is nonzero (the depth-0 guide does need it) — using the narrower value for
571	the marker's position while the box widens by the larger one drifted the marker left by
572	exactly `MARKER_RESERVE` (~12px at default sizes), confirmed by hand-deriving the algebra
573	and matching it against live computed-style output before fixing. Fixed by computing the
574	combined `extra` FIRST and using it for both the box widening AND the marker's own X
575	formula. New e2e test added specifically for this shape (heading → heading → paragraph,
576	three levels) rather than trusting the depth-0/depth-1 case again — verifies every
577	ancestor's marker center against its immediate child's guide column, not just the
578	top-level pair. A concrete, second instance of this project's own recurring lesson: a
579	real multi-level scenario is a different (and necessary) test from its simplest two-level
580	stand-in, and a screenshot a reviewer actually looked at caught what a passing assertion
581	suite didn't.
582	
583	**Follow-up: the native fold chevron overlapped our marker on headings, fixed with a
584	second, real multi-part bug of its own.** Reported directly in review: Obsidian's own
585	fold/collapse indicator (`.collapse-indicator.collapse-icon` — the actual glyph; its
586	wrapper, `.cm-fold-indicator`, measured 0 width live and isn't itself a useful reference)
587	sits in the exact same pixels our marker does, on every heading (confirmed live: the
588	chevron's own gutter is reserved even on a heading with no children yet, painted at
589	`opacity: 0` until hovered/foldable — so the reach is needed unconditionally, not only once
590	a heading actually has a child, which also avoids the marker jumping sideways the moment
591	one is added). Its own width doesn't vary by heading level (confirmed live, H1 through H3
592	identical in the bundled theme), unlike its vertical position, so it's measured ONCE per
593	render from any one heading line and applied uniformly — the same "measure a single
594	representative reference" pattern `nativeMarginBasePx` already established, not a new one.
595	
596	Fixed by folding this reach into the SAME `markerShortfall`/`--to-own-shift` mechanism
597	markers already use (not a parallel, independently-applied offset — an earlier draft of
598	this exact fix tried that shape and it silently double-subtracted). Two real bugs shipped
599	in getting there, both caught by review pushing on the result rather than by any
600	assertion in this experiment's own suite at the time:
601	
602	1. **Shifting a heading's own marker left of the chevron broke every DESCENDANT's guide
603	   column, which still pointed at the marker's OLD (un-shifted) center.** A first version
604	   only recomputed the fold-adjusted marker on the heading's OWN line, live, via
605	   `MarginCompensation` — correct for that one line in isolation, but guides (already fixed
606	   to align with a marker's CENTER, see above) are computed independently on every
607	   DESCENDANT line, with no way to know the ancestor's marker had moved. Root cause,
608	   structurally: only HEADINGS (and list items, which never own a guide at all) can fold in
609	   Obsidian's own UI — a `paragraph`-owned guide (this project's tree lets a paragraph have
610	   children too) never needs the extra reach, so the fix couldn't be a single per-line
611	   constant; it needed to know, PER ACTIVE GUIDE DEPTH on a given line, whether THAT
612	   specific depth's owning ancestor is a heading. Fixed by extending `computeLineGuides`
613	   (decorate.ts) with a new `headingGuideDepths` field — the subset of `guideDepths` whose
614	   owner is a heading — and generalizing `guideShortfall`/`guideLayer`/`guideBackground`/
615	   `combineExtra` to accept a per-depth fold-gap reach instead of one shared value. Checking
616	   only the shallowest active guide depth (the pre-existing optimization, valid without
617	   fold-gap since shortfall strictly decreases with depth) also had to go: fold-gap breaks
618	   that monotonicity, since it applies only to SOME depths — a deeper heading-owned guide
619	   can now need more reach than a shallower paragraph-owned one on the same line, so
620	   `combineExtra` checks every active depth, not just the first. The live-measurement pass
621	   in `MarginCompensation` was correspondingly widened from "the heading's own line only" to
622	   "every line that carries a marker OR bridges a heading-owned guide" — consolidated
623	   through one new shared function, `computeMarkerAndGuideBg`, so `lineDecoration()`,
624	   `gapLineDecoration()`, the widget loop, and this live pass all funnel through the exact
625	   same computation (foldGapPx defaulting to 0 for the three static callers) rather than
626	   four independently-maintained copies.
627	2. **The fix for bug 1 then dropped the `ownShiftUnits * unit` term for margin-shifted
628	   lines (atoms/list items) when recomputing `--to-own-shift` live**, caught by a dedicated
629	   e2e test (not a screenshot this time — the discrepancy was small enough, and the
630	   fixture specific enough, that a computed-style assertion was the right tool) failing by
631	   exactly one `--to-decor-unit` (24px at default sizes): `--to-own-shift` for these kinds
632	   must equal `ownShiftUnits * unit + extra` (their OWN depth-based margin shift, plus the
633	   marker/guide widening) — the new general live-override path set it to `extra` alone,
634	   which happens to be correct ONLY for block-kind lines (`ownShiftUnits === 0` there,
635	   invisible on a heading-only smoke test), silently wrong for anything margin-shifted.
636	   Fixed by reconstructing `ownShiftUnits` from the line's own fact (`isListItem` →
637	   `supplementalDepth`, `isAtom` → `depth`, else 0) the same way `lineDecoration()` already
638	   does, inside the live-override path too.
639	
640	New tests added for both: a heading-with-list-item-child fixture (the exact shape that
641	exposed bug 2) checking the marker/guide alignment invariant survives the fold-gap
642	interaction, plus the existing chevron-overlap check extended across three heading depths.
643	All pass; full suite green (127 unit tests, 71 e2e tests across all 8 spec files).
644	
645	**Experiment 5b close-out.** Final design: one uniform dot, `var(--text-faint)` (matching
646	the guide lines), on every eligible kind, positioned to clear both the node's own
647	indentation column and (for headings) the native fold chevron, vertically centered on the
648	node's own first rendered visual row, and horizontally centered on any bridging guide line
649	from a shallower ancestor. Eight real bugs were found and fixed across three follow-up
650	rounds after the initial "keep" verdict — none caught by this experiment's own fixture
651	corpus or assertions at the time they shipped; every one came from a reviewer looking at real
652	rendered output — a shared screenshot, in most cases — and pushing back on what they saw.
653	Total code cost stayed smaller than the initial 8-shape design despite three rounds
654	of bug fixes and the addition of a genuinely new live-measurement subsystem (Y-position,
655	fold-gap) that guides never needed — see the "Code cost" paragraph above and the
656	`combineExtra`/`computeMarkerAndGuideBg` consolidation in `decorations.ts` for why: fixing
657	each successive bug generally *simplified* the code by collapsing near-duplicate
658	computations into one shared function, not by adding new special cases. The two most
659	consequential lessons — recurring bug shapes, not one-off mistakes — are recorded as
660	their own entries in [11-decoration-lessons.md](11-decoration-lessons.md), since they apply
661	well beyond this one experiment.
662	
663	## Head-to-head: comparison and verdict (2026-07-19)
664	
665	**Verdict: 5a is the keeper.** PR #12 (5a) merged into the Experiment 5 integration branch;
666	PR #13 (5b) closed unmerged, with its results and lessons preserved here and in
667	[11-decoration-lessons.md](11-decoration-lessons.md).
668	
669	### The two branches diverged on two axes, not one
670	
671	The plan framed this as one head-to-head (icons vs. CSS shapes), but the experiments
672	actually diverged on two independent axes:
673	
674	- **Mechanism**: a real DOM element (5a: CM6 `Decoration.widget` in inline flow for plain
675	  lines, direct DOM injection for widget atoms) vs. one more background layer on the same
676	  `::after` pseudo-element the guides already use (5b).
677	- **Design**: 5a kept 8 distinct per-kind icons (plus a `markerVisibility` setting); 5b
678	  *pivoted mid-experiment* from 8 CSS-drawn shapes to a single uniform dot, after
679	  real-content review found the shape variety "cryptic, not helpful."
680	
681	Keeping the axes separate matters for interpreting the outcome: 5b's design pivot was a
682	verdict on *abstract CSS-gradient shapes* (lopsided pinwheels, a "⊥" for a plus-sign —
683	gradient recipes are hard to draw well and harder to distinguish at 60% opacity), not on
684	per-kind markers as such. 5a's real SVG icons are expressive and read clearly — our own
685	assessment after living with both. So the design question resolved *differently per
686	mechanism*, and the decisive comparison is chiefly about the mechanism.
687	
688	### Comparison table
689	
690	| Axis | 5a — SVG icons (DOM element) | 5b — uniform dot (background layer) |
691	|---|---|---|
692	| Final design | 8 distinct per-kind icons + `markerVisibility` setting (all / with-children / headings-and-paragraphs) | One `--text-faint` dot on every eligible kind (pivoted away from 8 shapes) |
693	| Mechanism | `Decoration.widget` in inline flow (plain lines) + DOM child injection (widget atoms) | One more background layer on the same `::after` guides use |
694	| Vertical alignment | **Free, by construction** — inline flow + `baseline` tracks the text's own font metrics at every heading level | Required a genuinely new live-measurement subsystem (`Range.getClientRects()[0]` per line, written to `--to-decor-marker-y`) |
695	| Coordinate coupling | Mild — marker gutter folded into `--to-own-shift` for atoms | Strong — markers and guides share one pseudo-element's coordinate space; produced 5b's most-repeated bug shape (3 shipped bugs from the same "two formulas must stay in sync" mistake, eventually consolidated into `computeMarkerAndGuideBg`) |
696	| Rendering quality | Crisp SVG at full opacity, own color | Inherits the guide rule's `opacity: 0.6` — dot reads visibly softer than text; **not fixable without moving to a DOM node** (i.e. adopting 5a's mechanism) |
697	| Text layout impact | Reserves a 1.25rem marker gutter on all non-list lines — text sits further right than the 2b baseline (depth-0 headings now have nonzero padding; two pre-existing tests deliberately updated) | Zero — dot lives in existing whitespace left of the indent column; text stays exactly at 2b's columns |
698	| Fold chevron conflict | Moved the *chevron* (static CSS transform on Obsidian's native element — a native-chrome touch, theme-fragile in principle) | Moved the *marker* — but this required per-ancestor-depth machinery (`headingGuideDepths`) because only heading-owned guides need the reach |
699	| Live measurement | Minimal (inherited `nativeMarginBasePx` etc. from 2b) | Substantial: Y-position, fold-gap, per-kind widget reference elements |
700	| Architecture | 3 ViewPlugins; nested-editor guard **included** (see 5a results, round 4) | StateField + 1 ViewPlugin; nested-editor guard **missing** (the leak was discovered on 5a after 5b concluded) |
701	| Code size | ~890-line decorations.ts, 240-line styles.css, + ~50 lines settings plumbing | ~890-line decorations.ts, 164-line styles.css |
702	| Extensibility | Wide open: per-kind color, hover states, future interactivity (it's a real element), visibility axis already shipped | Endpoint reached: any richer visual (full opacity, interaction, crisp shapes) pushes toward DOM anyway |
703	
704	### Why 5a wins on robustness, not just looks
705	
706	The headline surprise: **the "simple" variant didn't stay simpler.** 5b's premise was
707	"reuse proven plumbing, zero new mechanism" — and for the X axis that held. But vertical
708	centering on "the first rendered visual row" is fundamentally a *rendering* fact no
709	CSS-only formula can know (5b's own results establish this precisely, after two failed
710	CSS-only rounds), so 5b ended up building a live pixel-measurement subsystem anyway —
711	ironic, given 2b was originally chosen over 2a for "zero pixel measurement." Meanwhile
712	5a's inline-flow widget got vertical alignment for free from the browser's own text
713	layout. Both `decorations.ts` files converged to ~890 lines.
714	
715	5a's structural bets — inline-flow widget for plain lines, ViewPlugin injection for opaque
716	widgets, DOM-ancestry gating for nested editors — all held up under four rounds of
717	adversarial real-vault review. 5b's mechanism, by contrast, kept generating instances of
718	one recurring bug shape (the shared-coordinate-space divergence recorded in
719	[11-decoration-lessons.md](11-decoration-lessons.md)), and its one unfixable limitation
720	(markers share the guides' 0.6 opacity) can only be fixed by adopting 5a's mechanism.
721	
722	What 5b genuinely kept simpler: zero added DOM nodes, and zero text-layout impact (no
723	gutter — text stays exactly at the 2b baseline columns, where 5a shifts everything
724	non-list right by 1.25rem).
725	
726	**Asymmetry that decides it**: 5a's mechanism can trivially render 5b's design (a uniform
727	dot is just one more SVG icon, and the `markerVisibility` setting already exists to tune
728	where markers appear). The reverse is not true — 5b's mechanism cannot reach full opacity,
729	crisp shapes, or interactivity without becoming 5a.
730	
731	### What both experiments independently converged on
732	
733	Both branches arrived, through separate review rounds, at the same geometry: **the guide
734	line runs straight through the marker's center**, like a native nested list's connecting
735	line through its bullets (5a via placement exploration settling on "centered on the guide
736	column"; 5b by shifting guide columns to the marker centers). Both excluded list items
737	entirely. Both had to clear the native fold chevron. That convergence is itself a
738	validated design fact, independent of mechanism.
739	
740	### What carries over from 5b
741	
742	- The **design restraint lesson**: variety without established meaning reads as noise.
743	  5a's per-kind icons passed real-content review where 5b's abstract shapes didn't, but
744	  the burden of proof stays on variety — the `markerVisibility` setting exists precisely
745	  because even good icons can be more than a leaf node needs.
746	- The **guide-through-marker-center geometry** (already independently present in 5a).
747	- **Five cross-experiment findings** (shared-value coupling, `background-position` `%`
748	  semantics, measure-the-glyph-not-the-wrapper, per-depth vs. per-line adjustments,
749	  simplification-as-legitimate-outcome) — merged into
750	  [11-decoration-lessons.md](11-decoration-lessons.md).
751	- The **live chevron measurement** approach — 5b measured the fold chevron's reach live
752	  per render; 5a hardcoded two measured constants. Porting 5b's approach is hardening
753	  item 1 below.
754	
755	### Cross-branch bug alert (important beyond this experiment)
756	
757	5a's round 4 discovered that decorations leak into Obsidian's **nested per-cell table
758	editors** (a bare word in an actively-edited cell classifies as a "paragraph" and picks up
759	indentation + a marker *inside the cell*). The fix (ViewPlugins gated on a DOM-ancestry
760	`isNestedEditor()` check) exists **only on 5a**. The latent leak affects the un-merged 5b
761	*and the upstream 2b/1 branches* (indentation alone leaks, markers just made it obvious).
762	No action needed on the chosen path — 5a carries the fix and everything merges through it
763	— but if the upstream experiment branches are ever revived independently, they need the
764	same guard.
765	
766	## Next steps: hardening 5a
767	
768	Concerns identified in the post-experiment code review (2026-07-19), ranked. None are
769	architecture-threatening; they're hardening tasks, invariants to protect, and deferred
770	polish for when 5a graduates from experiment to the real implementation.
771	
772	1. **Replace the two hardcoded fold-chevron constants with live measurement.** The chevron
773	   repositioning is a static CSS transform (`translateX(calc(-1 * (var(--to-marker-gutter)
774	   + 0.425rem - 3px)))`) where `0.425rem` is half the chevron wrapper's measured 22px width
775	   and `3px` is measured internal dead space. This is the one place 5a violates the
776	   project's own "read native values live instead of hardcoding compensations" rule
777	   ([11-decoration-lessons.md](11-decoration-lessons.md)); a theme or Obsidian update that
778	   resizes the chevron silently degrades the layout (cosmetic-only failure). 5b's session
779	   solved the same problem with live measurement — port that approach.
780	2. **Protect two documented invariants in code review.** (a) DOM injection into widget-atom
781	   subtrees works because Obsidian never re-diffs those opaque subtrees — an undocumented
782	   invariant; failure mode on an Obsidian change is re-injection flicker or duplicated
783	   markers. (b) Never append children into a plain `.cm-line` — confirmed to peg the
784	   renderer at 100%+ CPU via CM6's mutation-observer feedback loop (documented in
785	   `decorations.ts`'s module comment). Both must survive future refactors.
786	3. **Evaluate `app.workspace.updateOptions()` as a replacement for the `forceRedraw`
787	   off/on mode-toggle hack** (used to refresh `MarginCompensation` after a settings change
788	   when decoration output is byte-identical). The hack works and its reasoning is sound,
789	   but toggling a user-visible mode as an internal refresh is fragile if mode toggling
790	   ever gains side effects. `updateOptions()` is Obsidian's public API for exactly
791	   "editor-extension-affecting settings changed" — and obsidian-lapel (see the prior-art
792	   addendum below) confirms the pattern works in the wild: it swaps the registered
793	   extension array's entry in place and makes this one call.
794	4. **Consolidate to one shared `parse()`/`decorate()` pass per transaction.** Currently
795	   three ViewPlugins each re-parse the full document on every update (the 2b baseline did
796	   it twice). Same asymptotics, tripled constant; fine for normal notes, worth
797	   consolidating before testing against multi-thousand-line files. Decoration sets are
798	   also built for the whole document, not the viewport — a further (deferred) option;
799	   obsidian-lapel demonstrates the standard viewport-limited shape (build only over
800	   `view.viewport`, rebuild on `docChanged || viewportChanged`), and additionally shows
801	   that per-line *kind* facts can come from CM6's own incremental `syntaxTree` (via
802	   `lineClassNodeProp`) with no separate reparse at all — not a fit for our own tree
803	   depths (our universal-tree semantics aren't in CM6's grammar), but potentially a fit
804	   for the kind-classification part of the work.
805	5. **Resolve the `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd` violations 5a introduced.** The marker
806	   widget's inline styles trip `obsidianmd/no-static-styles-assignment` (14 errors) and
807	   `obsidianmd/prefer-create-el` — the official lint rules this project's "perfect
808	   scorecard" bar (decision Q1) commits to satisfying. Static styles should move to CSS
809	   classes; genuinely dynamic ones (`left`, per-kind expressions) to `setCssProps`. Found
810	   by `npm run lint` on the merged branch, 2026-07-19; pre-existing warnings aside, the
811	   errors are all in 5a's marker code.
812	6. **Polish**: `aria-hidden="true"` on the marker SVGs (decorative, screen readers should
813	   skip them); untested contexts — RTL text, IME composition at line start, community
814	   themes beyond those exercised.
815	7. **Accepted design costs, restated so they aren't rediscovered as "bugs" later**: every
816	   non-list line reserves the 1.25rem marker gutter, so text visibly shifts when toggling
817	   outline mode; two Experiment-1 invariants were knowingly relaxed (depth-0 lines are no
818	   longer padding-free; same-depth list items vs. atoms no longer share a column). The
819	   pure-list invariant (a list with no non-list ancestors renders byte-identical to
820	   outline-mode-off) still holds — list items reserve nothing.
821	
822	## Prior-art addendum: obsidian-lapel (2026-07-19)
823	
824	Reviewed after the head-to-head verdict, before calling the experiment wrapped:
825	[obsidian-lapel](https://github.com/liamcain/obsidian-lapel) (by Liam Cain, an Obsidian
826	team member) ships a related idea — per-level heading markers ("H1"…"H6") with a
827	click-to-change-level menu. Four source files, so the whole implementation was read.
828	
829	**Mechanism: a third approach neither 5a nor 5b tried.** Lapel renders markers in a CM6
830	`gutter()` — a dedicated column left of the content area, ordered relative to the
831	line-number gutter via extension precedence (`Prec.high`/`Prec.low`). Because a gutter
832	marker lives entirely outside `.cm-content`, *every* hard problem this experiment fought
833	simply doesn't exist there: no text-layout impact, no gutter reservation, no
834	`contain: paint`, no fold-chevron collision, no blockquote `::before` conflict, no
835	readable-line-width margin interaction. The trade-off is structural: a gutter is a flat,
836	fixed column — it cannot place a marker at the node's own indent depth, which is the whole
837	point of our design (the marker as a crown on the guide line, at the node's own column).
838	So it doesn't replace 5a's mechanism for us, but it is the right tool for flat per-line
839	chrome, worth remembering if we ever add any (e.g. node handles, diagnostics).
840	
841	**Independent confirmation of the nested-editor leak (5a's round 4).** Lapel hit the exact
842	same bug class: its gutter renders inside Obsidian's per-table-cell nested editors. Their
843	fix is a CSS hide (`.table-cell-wrapper .cm-gutters { display: none }`) with a comment
844	calling it a hack to remove "once there's a proper way to not register the editor
845	extension inside table cells" — i.e. they too found no official API to scope
846	`registerEditorExtension`, and their workaround only hides the symptom (the extension
847	still runs). Our DOM-ancestry `isNestedEditor()` gate is structurally stronger: it stops
848	the computation, not just the paint.
849	
850	**Confirmations picked up into the hardening checklist** (items 3 and 4 above):
851	`app.workspace.updateOptions()` as the settings-refresh mechanism, viewport-limited
852	building, and CM6's own `syntaxTree` as a kind-classification source.
853	
854	### Potential follow-ups (deliberately out of Experiment 5's scope)
855	
856	- **Per-level heading markers (H1–H6).** Considered during the experiment but kept out to
857	  avoid scope creep; lapel validates the idea in the wild. Under 5a's mechanism this is
858	  small: carry the heading `level` (already on `OutlineNode`, present iff
859	  `kind === 'heading'`) through `LineDecorationFact`, and either branch
860	  `buildMarkerIcon` per level or render a small text label. Lapel's customization
861	  pattern is worth copying regardless of the visual: a `data-level` attribute plus a
862	  CSS-custom-property indirection (`--heading-marker`, consumed by `content:`) lets
863	  themes/snippets restyle markers per level without touching the plugin — our markers
864	  could expose `data-kind` (and `data-level`) the same way.
865	- **Marker interactivity.** Lapel's markers are clickable: a `Menu` listing heading
866	  levels 1–6 (checked state on the current level, `lucide-heading-N` icons) plus a
867	  "Body" option, dispatching a line rewrite. This is exactly the "future interactivity"
868	  potential the head-to-head credited 5a's real-DOM mechanism with — a node's marker as
869	  a click target for outline operations (change kind/level, fold, zoom, structural
870	  moves). Two caveats from lapel's own code: our `MarkerWidget` currently sets
871	  `pointer-events: none` + `ignoreEvent() { return true }`, both of which would need
872	  revisiting carefully (CM6 widget event handling interacts with editor focus/cursor);
873	  and lapel positions its menu via `Menu.setParentElement`, which is NOT public API
874	  (they augment `obsidian.d.ts` locally) — a public-API-only equivalent
875	  (`showAtMouseEvent` alone) needs verifying against our bar first.
876	
877	## Open question: shrinking only our own added list margin
878	
879	Raised during Experiment 1's review, not yet decided. The deferred list-hang issue (see [Experiment 1's results](08-experiment-1-additive-indentation.md)) is native
880	Obsidian chrome and explicitly out of scope for direct edits in this experiment. But a
881	narrower variant stays inside the additive-only discipline: **reduce only the margin *we*
882	add** to list items — not native `text-indent`/`padding-left` — by the list's own native
883	hang width, read live via `getComputedStyle` the same way Experiment 1's table fix (bug #3 in
884	[08-experiment-1-additive-indentation.md](08-experiment-1-additive-indentation.md))
885	reads and compensates for native padding. Worth exploring as a follow-up, with two open
886	risks to resolve before trying it: (a) clamping so a shallow `supplementalDepth` never goes
887	negative once the hang is subtracted (the table fix's `max(0px, ...)` pattern applies
888	directly), and (b) the compensation must be based on the list **root**'s own hang, not each
889	item's — nested items can have wider markers (e.g. `10.` vs `-`) with different native hang
890	widths, and compensating per-item instead of per-root-chain would reintroduce exactly the
891	kind of within-list misalignment the wide-numbering fixture exists to catch.
892
